subject: Computer Networking
title: IPv6 Adoption
description: A fast breakdown of IPv6 Adoption for builders.

hook.text: IPv4 has 4.3 billion addresses. The world has 20+ billion connected devices. The math stopped working years ago.

buildup.text: IPv6 was finalized in 1998 and offers 340 undecillion addresses. Enough for every atom on Earth to have billions.

discovery.text: IPv6 simplifies headers, removes NAT, and has built-in support for auto-configuration and security.

twist.text: Despite decades of readiness, IPv6 adoption is around 40% globally. NAT made IPv4 last far longer than expected.

climax.text: Mobile networks lead adoption — most 4G and 5G networks run IPv6 natively. The transition continues slowly.

punchline.text: IPv6 is the future. It's just arriving slower than planned.

quiz.question: Why has IPv6 adoption been slow despite IPv4 address exhaustion?
options: NAT extended IPv4's life far beyond expectations
IPv6 is less secure than IPv4
